summary.title: Summary
summary.description: Summary metadata must be completed by Data Custodians onboarding metadata into the Innovation Gateway MVP.

documentation.title: Documentation
documentation.description: Documentation can include a rich text description of the dataset or links to media such as documents, images, presentations, videos or links to data dictionaries, profiles or dashboards. Organisations are required to confirm that they have permission to distribute any additional media.

coverage.title: Coverage
coverage.description: This information includes attributes for geographical and temporal coverage, cohort details etc. to enable a deeper understanding of the dataset content so that researchers can make decisions about the relevance of the underlying data.

provenance.title: Provenance
provenance.description: Provenance information allows researchers to understand data within the context of its origins and can be an indicator of quality, authenticity and timeliness.

accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.description: Accessibility information allows researchers to understand access, usage, limitations, formats, standards and linkage or interoperability with toolsets.

enrichmentAndLinkage.title: Enrichment and Linkage
enrichmentAndLinkage.description: This section includes information about related datasets, tools and collections on the gateway and elsewhere. If a dataset has been enriched and/or derivations, scores and existing tools are available this section allows providers to indicate this to researchers.

structuralMetadata.title: Structural Metadata
structuralMetadata.description: Descriptions of all tables and data elements that can be included in the dataset

url.description: URL endpoint to obtain the version

title.$comment: dct:title. title is reserved word in json schema
title.title: Title
title.description: Title of the dataset limited to 80 characters. It should provide a short description of the dataset and be unique across the gateway. If your title is not unique, please add a prefix with your organisation name or identifier to differentiate it from other datasets within the Gateway. Please avoid acronyms wherever possible. Good titles should summarise the content of the dataset and if relevant, the region the dataset covers.
examples: North West London COVID-19 Patient Level Situation Report

abstract.title: Dataset Abstract
abstract.description: Provide a clear and brief descriptive signpost for researchers who are searching for data that may be relevant to their research. The abstract should allow the reader to determine the scope of the data collection and accurately summarise its content. The optimal length is one paragraph (limited to 255 characters) and effective abstracts should avoid long sentences and abbreviations where possible
examples: CPRD Aurum contains primary care data contributed by General Practitioner (GP) practices using EMIS Web® including patient registration information and all care events that GPs have chosen to record as part of their usual medical practice.

shortTitle.title: Short Dataset Title
examples: NELA - instead of National Emergency Laparotomy Audit
shortTitle.description: Where it exists, please provide a known short alternative title for the dataset.

custodianDatasetID.title: Custodian's Dataset ID
custodianDatasetID.description: An ID or reference used by the data custodian to identify the dataset within their own catalogue or records. Field will be searchable, but not visible to researchers.

securityManager.title: Security Manager
securityManager.default: Defaults to empty content, to allow completion at a later date. Must be explicitly stated
securityManager.description: Please provide a name and contact details for party responsible for dataset security. Organisations are expected to provide a dedicated email address associated with data security process. Notes- An employee's email address can only be provided on a temporary basis and if one is provided an explicit consent must be obtained for this purpose.

integrityManager.title: Integrity Manager
integrityManager.default: Defaults to empty content, to allow completion at a later date. Must be explicitly stated
integrityManager.description: Please provide a name and contact details for party responsible for ensuring the integrity for the dataset. Organisations are expected to provide a dedicated email address associated with data security process. Notes- An employee's email address can only be provided on a temporary basis and if one is provided an explicit consent must be obtained for this purpose.

accessManager.title: Access Manager
accessManager.default: Defaults to empty content, to allow completion at a later date. Must be explicitly stated
accessManager.description: Please provide a name and contact details for party responsible for access to the dataset. Organisations are expected to provide a dedicated email address associated with data security process. Notes- An employee's email address can only be provided on a temporary basis and if one is provided an explicit consent must be obtained for this purpose.

keywords.title: Keywords
keywords.description: Please provide relevant and specific keywords from controlled vocabularities where suitable. These will improve the discoverability of your dataset. Where a suitable keyword cannot be identified, please request additions by contacting HDRUK

doi.title: Digital Object Identifier
doi.description: All HDR UK registered datasets should either have a Digital Object Identifier (DOI) or be working towards obtaining one. If a DOI is available, please provide the DOI.

datasetType.title: Dataset Type
datasetType.description: A category describing how data was collected and stored

organisation.title: Organisation Metadata
organisation.description: Describes an organisation for purposes of discovery and identification.

identifier.title: Organisation Identifier
identifier.description: Please provide a Grid.ac identifier (see https://www.grid.ac/institutes) for your organisation. If your organisation does not have a Grid.ac identifier please use the “suggest and institute” function here: https://www.grid.ac/institutes#

logo.title: Organisation Logo
logo.description: Please provide a logo associated with the Gateway Organisation using a valid URL. The following formats will be accepted .jpg, .png or .svg.

description.title: Organisation Description
description.description: Please provide a URL that describes the organisation.

memberOf.title: Organisation Membership
memberOf.description: Please indicate if the organisation is an Alliance Member or a Hub.

accessRights.title: Organisation Default Access Rights
accessRights.description: The URL of a webpage where the data access request process and/or guidance is provided. If there is more than one access process i.e. industry vs academic please provide both.

deliveryLeadTime.title: Access Request Duration
deliveryLeadTime.description: Please provide an indication of the typical processing times based on the types of requests typically received. Note: This value will be used as default access request duration for all datasets submitted by the organisation. However, there will be the opportunity to overwrite this value for each dataset.

accessService.title: Organisation Access Service
accessService.description: Please provide a brief description of the data access services that are available including: environment that is currently available to researchers;additional consultancy and services;any indication of costs associated. If no environment is currently available, please indicate the current plans and timelines when and how data will be made available to researchers Note: This value will be used as default access environment for all datasets submitted by the organisation. However, there will be the opportunity to overwrite this value for each dataset.

accessRequestCost.title: Organisation Access Request Cost
accessRequestCost.description: Please provide link(s) to a webpage or a short description detailing the commercial model for processing data access requests for the organisation (if available) Definition: Indication of commercial model or cost (in GBP) for processing each data access request by the data custodian.

description.title: Description
description.description: A free-text description of the record.

associatedMedia.title: Associated Media
associatedMedia.description: Please provide any media associated with the Gateway Organisation using a valid URI for the content. This is an opportunity to provide additional context that could be useful for researchers wanting to understand more about the dataset and its relevance to their research question. The following formats will be accepted .jpg, .png or .svg, .pdf, .xslx or .docx. Note: media asset can be hosted by the organisation or uploaded using the onboarding portal.

physicalSampleAvailability.title: Physical Sample Availability
physicalSampleAvailability.$comment: No standard identified. Used enumeration from the UK Tissue Directory.
physicalSampleAvailability.description: Availability of physical samples associated with the dataset. If samples are available, please indicate the types of samples that are available. More than one type may be provided. If sample are not yet available, please provide “AVAILABILITY TO BE CONFIRMED”. If samples are not available, then please provide “NOT AVAILABLE”.

spatial.title: Geographic Coverage
spatial.description: The geographical area covered by the dataset. It is recommended that links are to entries in a well-maintained gazetteer such as https://www.geonames.org/ or https://what3words.com/daring.lion.race.

typicalAgeRange.title: Age Range
typicalAgeRange.description: Please indicate the age range of subjects in the dataset.

followup.title: Follow Up Time
followup.description: If known, what is the typical time span that a patient appears in the dataset (follow up period)

pathway.title: Pathway
pathway.description: Please indicate if the dataset is representative of the patient pathway and any limitations the dataset may have with respect to pathway coverage. This could include if the dataset is from a single speciality or area, a single tier of care, linked across two tiers (e.g. primary and secondary care), or an integrated care record covering the whole patient pathway.

collectionSituation.title: Setting
collectionSituation.description: Pleases indicate the setting(s) where data was collected. Multiple settings may be provided

accrualPeriodicity.title: Periodicity
accrualPeriodicity.description: Please indicate the frequency of distribution release. If a dataset is distributed regularly please choose a distribution release periodicity from the constrained list and indicate the next release date. When the release date becomes historical, a new release date will be calculated based on the publishing periodicity. If a dataset has been published and will remain static please indicate that it is static and indicated when it was released. If a dataset is released on an irregular basis or “on-demand” please indicate that it is Irregular and leave release date as null. If a dataset can be published in real-time or near-real-time please indicate that it is continuous and leave release date as null. Notes: see https://www.dublincore.org/specifications/dublin-core/collection-description/frequency/

distributionReleaseDate.title: Release Date
distributionReleaseDate.description: Date of the latest release of the dataset. If this is a regular release i.e. quarterly, or this is a static dataset please complete this alongside Periodicity. If this is Irregular or Continuously released please leave this blank. Notes: Periodicity and release date will be used to determine when the next release is expected. E.g. if the release date is documented as 01/01/2020 and it is now 20/04/2020 and there is a quarterly release schedule, the latest release will be calculated as 01/04/2020.

startDate.title: Start Date
startDate.description: The start of the time period that the dataset provides coverage for. If there are multiple cohorts in the dataset with varying start dates, please provide the earliest date and use the description or the media attribute to provide more information.

endDate.title: End Date
endDate.description: The end of the time period that the dataset provides coverage for. If the dataset is 'continuous' and has no known end date, please state continuous. If there are multiple cohorts in the dataset with varying end dates, please provide the latest date and use the description or the media attribute to provide more information.

timeLag.title: Time Lag
timeLag.description: Please indicate the typical range in time-lag between an event and the data for that event appearing in the dataset

usage.title: Usage
usage.description: This section includes information about how the data can be used and how it is currently being used

access.title: Access
access.description: This section includes information about data access

formatAndStandards.title: Format and Standards
formatAndStandards.description: Section includes technical attributes for language vocabularies, sizes etc. and gives researchers facts about and processing the underlying data in the dataset.

dataUseLimitation.title: Data Use Limitation
dataUseLimitation.description: Please provide an indication of consent permissions for datasets and/or materials, and relates to the purposes for which datasets and/or material might be removed, stored or used. NOTE: we have extended the DUO to include a value for NO LINKAGE

dataUseRequirements.title: Data Use Requirements
dataUseRequirements.description: Please indicate fit here are any additional conditions set for use if any, multiple requirements may be provided. Please ensure that these restrictions are documented in access rights information.

resourceCreator.title: Citation Requirements
resourceCreator.description: Please provide the text that you would like included as part of any citation that credits this dataset. This is typically just the name of the publisher.   No employee details should be provided.

isReferencedBy.title: Citations
isReferencedBy.description: Please provide the citations for publications associated with the dataset. Citations should links to existing resources where the dataset has been used or referenced.

accessRights.title: Access Rights
accessRights.$comment: dct:access_rights NOTE: need to ensure that this is consistent across the organisation info and the dataset info

accessService.title: Access Service
accessService.description: Please provide a brief description of the data access services that are available including: environment that is currently available to researchers;additional consultancy and services;any indication of costs associated. If no environment is currently available, please indicate the current plans and timelines when and how data will be made available to researchers Note: This value will be used as default access environment for all datasets submitted by the organisation. However, there will be the opportunity to overwrite this value for each dataset.

accessRequestCost.title: Organisation Access Request Cost
accessRequestCost.description: Please provide link(s) to a webpage detailing the commercial model for processing data access requests for the organisation (if available) Definition: Indication of commercial model or cost (in GBP) for processing each data access request by the data custodian.

deliveryLeadTime.title: Access Request Duration
deliveryLeadTime.description: Please provide an indication of the typical processing times based on the types of requests typically received.

jurisdiction.title: Jurisdiction
jurisdiction.$comment: http://purl.org/dc/terms/Jurisdiction FIXME: Add ISO 3166-2 Subdivision code pattern
jurisdiction.description: Please use country code from ISO 3166-1 country codes and the associated ISO 3166-2 for regions, cities, states etc. for the country/state under whose laws the data subjects' data is collected, processed and stored.

dataController.title: Data Controller
dataController.description: Data Controller means a person/entity who (either alone or jointly or in common with other persons/entities) determines the purposes for which and the way any Data Subject data, specifically personal data or are to be processed.

dataProcessor.title: Data Processor
dataProcessor.description: A Data Processor, in relation to any Data Subject data, specifically personal data, means any person/entity (other than an employee of the data controller) who processes the data on behalf of the data controller.

conformsTo.title: Conforms To
conformsTo.description: List standardised data models that the dataset has been stored in or transformed to, such as OMOP or FHIR. If the data is only available in a local format, please make that explicit. If you are using a standard that has not been included in the list, please use “other” and contact support desk to ask for an addition.

language.title: Language
language.description: This should list all the languages in which the dataset metadata and underlying data is made available.
language.$comment: dct:language. FIXME: Conforms to spec, but may be a list of strings given cardinality 1:*. Validate against external list of languages. Resources defined by the Library of Congress (ISO 639-1, ISO 639-2) SHOULD be used.

format.title: Format
format.description: If multiple formats are available please specify. See application, audio, image, message, model, multipart, text, video, https://www.iana.org/assignments/media-types/media-types.xhtml Note: If your file format is not included in the current list of formats, please indicate other. If you are using the HOP you will be directed to a service desk page where you can request your additional format. If not please go to: https://metadata.atlassian.net/servicedesk/customer/portal/4 to request your format.

datasetLinks.title: Linked Datasets
datasetLinks.description: Where possible please provide references to other datasets, tools or collections on the gateway which have been linked to this dataset.

tools.title: Tools
tools.$comment: No standard identified. We encourage users to adopt a model along the lines of https://www.ga4gh.org/news/tool-registry-service-api-enabling-an-interoperable-library-of-genomics-analysis-tools/
tools.description: Please provide the URL of any analysis tools or models that have been created for this dataset and are available for further use. Multiple tools may be provided. Note: We encourage users to adopt a model along the lines of https://www.ga4gh.org/news/tool-registry-service-api-enabling-an-interoperable-library-of-genomics-analysis-tools/

tableStructure.title: Table Level Data
tableStructure.description: Please provide information about each table in the dataset

mediaSubType.title: Media Subtype
mediaSubType.description: A more granular description of media type

isocountrycode.$comment: FIXME: Add ISO 3166-2 Subdivision code pattern

name.title: Contact Name
name.description: The name of the contact department, group or - if necessary - a specific person.

email.title: Contact Email
email.description: The email of the contact department, group or - if necessary - a specific person.

phone.title: Contact Phone Number
phone.description: The main phone number for the contact department, group or - if necessary - a specific person

notes.title: Contact Notes
notes.description: Notes providing extra detail about the contact

type.title: Keyword Type
type.description: The controlled vocabulary from which the keyword was chosen - or 'Free'
type.$comment: Enum controlled - types to be added as controlled vocabs grow

value.title: Keyword
value.description: A keyword chosen from extended controlled vocabularies

isExtended.title: Extended Vocabulary
isExtended.description: Indication of whether keyword was chosen from standard controlled vocabularies or has extended a controlled vocabulary after request to HDR UK

lowerValue.title: Lower Value
lowerValue.description: The lowest value in the time Range

lowerUnits.title: Lower Units
lowerUnits.description: The unit used with the lowest value in the timeRange

upperValue.title: Upper  Value
upperValue.description: The lowest value in the timeRange

upperUnits.title: Upper Units
upperUnits.description: The unit used with the lowest value in the timeRange

isContinuous.title: Continuous Update Flag
isContinuous.description: Flag is true when the dataset is frquently updated and thus upper value is consistently changing - deemed less meaningful

authors.title: Lower Value
authors.description: A list of authors cited by the publication

title.title: Paper Title
title.description: The title of the paper or article

publicationName.title: Publication Title
publicationName.description: The title of the publication / journal the paper is written for

tableName.title: tableName
tableName.description: Name of the table, unique within the dataset

rowCount.title: Row Count
rowCount.description: The number of rows in the table

columnCount.title: Column Count
columnCount.description: The number of columns in the table

primaryKey.title: Primary Key
primaryKey.description: Primary Key found within this table
primaryKey.$comment: Please provide the column name of the primary key for this table.

foreignKeys.title: Foreign Keys
foreignKeys.description: Foreign keys found within this table
foreignKeys.$comment: Please provide the column names of any foreign keys found within this table, which maybe found in other tables.
